findingClasses: F1: Parser non-determinism for multi-phrase inputs (range interactionMap is randomized; multi-phrase inputs like 'made it then tried it later' returned different InteractionType values across runs)

F2: Lost-update race on artifacts.relevance_score (updateRelevanceFromAnnotation did SELECT current → compute → UPDATE new without serialization; concurrent annotations against the same artifact dropped deltas)
implementationSurfaces: internal/annotation/parser.go (sort import + sortedInteractionPhrasesList cached length-desc + alphabetical-tiebreaker slice; Parse loop iterates the sorted slice instead of the randomized map)

internal/intelligence/annotations.go (single atomic UPDATE artifacts SET relevance_score = LEAST(GREATEST(COALESCE(relevance_score, 0.5) + $1, 0), 1) WHERE id = $2 RETURNING relevance_score; exported ApplyAnnotationRelevanceForTest wrapper)

testExecution.integrationCompile: go build -tags integration ./tests/integration/... -> CLEAN; go vet -tags integration ./tests/integration/... -> CLEAN

rounds.note: Annotation pipeline stability hardening — two latent defects (parser non-determinism and SELECT→compute→UPDATE lost-update race) closed with persistent regression coverage (unit + integration tests committed). Bounded-range clamp moved from application code into SQL (LEAST/GREATEST/COALESCE) — defence-in-depth even if writers were not serialized.

findingClasses: F1: docs/Operations.md Scope 5 metric reference table (line 2616) described smackerel_qf_engagement_signal_attempts_total as 'pre-MVP placeholder: registered with zero emissions until Scope 6 transport lands' — operator-runbook drift because Scope 6 transport DID land per state.json (Scope 6 certified Done 2026-05-22T19:50Z) with 6 emission call-sites in internal/connector/qfdecisions/engagement.go:558,693,769,795,813,820 (overflow_drop, two degraded after-retry-budget, two accepted on 201/200, one rejected on 4xx). Operators reading the Scope 5 section would believe the metric is silent when it is actively emitted.
rounds.closurePatternRationale: Single-line operator-runbook documentary drift in docs/Operations.md. No spec/scope/test/source-code/behavior change required. The devops-to-doc parent owns the docs phase directly; no bug packet artifact set needed because there is no defect under existing spec semantics — the metric was correctly registered by Scope 5 and is correctly emitted by Scope 6. Only the Scope 5 reference table cell needed to be brought into line with the post-Scope-6 reality. The 4 spec 041 carry-forward concerns (C-CLOSEOUT-S5-S8-G022-RESERVED-PHASES, C-CLOSEOUT-S2-S5-CONSUMER-TRACE-PLANNING, C-CLOSEOUT-S5-G028-FAKE-INTEGRATION-RENDER-GO-PRE-EXISTING, C-CLOSEOUT-QF-063-PRODUCER-WIRING-CROSS-PRODUCT-MILESTONE) are untouched and the spec stays at done_with_concerns.

rounds.note: Devops surface for spec 041 (QF Companion Connector) probed end-to-end across 11 surfaces. QF integration is an outbound HTTP client — no hosted image, no extra service, no deploy-target adapter changes required. Build pipeline, deploy contract, compose wiring, SST env vars, tailnet-edge bind, rollback contract, and packet metadata preservation are all CLEAN. Single finding F1 was a one-line stale text in docs/Operations.md Scope 5 metric reference table describing the engagement-signal metric as a pre-MVP placeholder even though Scope 6 had delivered the transport — closed via R8-lightweight-parent-only-docs-edit (no bug packet, no spec status change). Spec 041 remains done_with_concerns with the existing 4 carry-forward concerns untouched. State-transition-guard reports 16 BLOCKs all mapping to documented carry-forward concerns (no new BLOCKs). Round counts as 1 finding discovered + 1 finding closed.
